seoTitle: How to Export Data From Trello (JSON & CSV)
metaDescription: Learn how to export your Trello board data as JSON or CSV for backups, archives, or spreadsheet reporting, plus a workaround for free accounts.
intro: Backing up a Trello board or preparing a spreadsheet summary for stakeholders is easier once you know where the export options live. Trello lets you pull your board data as a raw JSON file or, on some plans, directly as a CSV. This guide walks through finding the export menu, choosing the right format, and opening your data in a spreadsheet app.

steps.heading: Open your board's menu
steps.body: Navigate to the board you want to export, click the three-dot menu icon in the top corner, and scroll to the More options link.

steps.heading: Find the export panel
steps.body: In the submenu, click Print, Export, and Share to open the export options for that board.

steps.heading: Export as JSON
steps.body: Click Export as JSON to open a raw data payload in a new browser tab, then use Save As to store it as a backup file.

steps.heading: Export as CSV or use the URL workaround
steps.body: If your plan supports it, select Export as CSV for a direct spreadsheet download; otherwise you can try appending .csv to the export URL to force a download.

steps.heading: Review your exported data
steps.body: Open the file in Excel or Google Sheets and check that columns like descriptions, due dates, and labels loaded correctly, then save it to a dedicated backup folder.

faq.question: Is CSV export available on the free Trello plan?
faq.answer: Direct CSV export is generally a premium feature, though free users can still export JSON and, in some cases, use a URL-based workaround.

faq.question: What data is included in a Trello export?
faq.answer: An export typically includes your card structures, checklists, list arrangements, member tags, and custom fields.

faq.question: Can I import an exported JSON file back into Trello?
faq.answer: Trello's own import tools primarily support CSV, so a JSON export is best treated as a backup rather than something you re-import directly.

faq.question: How often should I back up my board data?
faq.answer: It depends on how active the board is, but setting a regular schedule, like monthly, helps protect your team's historical records.
